revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/bunkazai/065.html Kofu city official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/maizou-bnk/topics/401_500/0477.html Yamanashi Prefecture official home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]]

The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]]

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|Artifacts from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|burial goods from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|35|36|N|138|34|49|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:大丸山古墳出土 革綴短甲・有柄鉄斧.JPG|right|thumb|burial goods from the Ōmaruyama Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[sarcophagus]] was painted in [[vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/bunkazai/065.html Kofu city official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/maizou-bnk/topics/401_500/0477.html Yamanashi Prefecture official home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.

The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11431880]] Wikidata name: Ōmaruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.59225|N|138.582917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11431880]] Wikidata name: Ōmaruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.59225|N|138.582917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11431880]] Wikidata name: Ōmaruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.59225|N|138.582917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|WD=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River}}. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu}} and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}}. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|WD=Q6573293|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/bunkazai/065.html Kofu city official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/maizou-bnk/topics/401_500/0477.html Yamanashi Prefecture official home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[en:Ōmaruyama Kofun]][[ja:大丸山古墳]]
{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.59225|N|138.582917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|WD=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River}}. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu}} and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}}. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|WD=Q6573293|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)}}

One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}} and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|WD=Q6573293|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)}}

{{Short description|4th century Kofun period tumulus in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmaruyama Kofun | native_name = 大丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Omaruyama-kofun enkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmaruyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.59225|N|138.582917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmaruyama Kofun'''|大丸山古墳|}} is a 4th century [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} located in the Nakamichi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu, Yamanashi]] in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/208367 |title= 大丸山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|WD=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River|1=Fuefuki River}}. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}} and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|WD=Q6573293|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)}}

==Overview== The Ōmaruyama Kofun is located on the southeastern edge of the Kōfu Basin on the ridge of a 310 meter mountain which extends to the left bank of the {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|WD=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River|1=Fuefuki River}}. The area has a high concentration of Kofun period remains and is located immediately above the [[Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Chōshizuka Kofun - Maruyamazuka Kofun]]. The Ōmaruyama Kofun was discovered in 1920, and is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has an overall length of 105 meters, and is aligned in along an east-west axis, taking advantage of the natural topography. The posterior circular portion has a diameter 48 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 34 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1970 and again in 1976, during which time the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was uncovered and found to be a peculiar two-tied structure with length of 2.2 meters and width of 0.8 meters. The interior of the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]], and contained the remains of two adult men and women along with many [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. These included an almost intact set of armor made from iron plates, iron weapons and swords, agricultural implements, three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, beads and jewelry. One of the bronze mirrors, or ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' was of especial interest, as it was identical to mirrors found at the Uchikoshi Kofun in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}} and the [[Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}}. Many of the excavated artifacts are now preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|WD=Q6573293|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamanashi)}}
